title: 7 Submitting Electronic Bill Payments to Central 1 via File Transmission
body_text:
7	Submitting Electronic Bill Payments to Central 1 via File Transmission
7.1	Overview of Electronic Bill Payments Sent via File Transmission
Financial institutions can send bill payments to Central 1 via file transmission or direct connection to Central 1. Bill payments submitted via file transmission are sent as batch files for financial institutions in Ontario.
Financial institutions must ensure that their electronic bill payment files are backed up and available if required.
7.2	Electronic Bill Payment File Specifications
Bill payment file specifications are provided to the financial institution during enrollment to the Electronic Bill Payments Service. Contact Central 1 as directed in Section 1.5, Contacts and Websites, for more information.
7.3	Uploading Electronic Bill Payment Files to Central 1
Bill payments are forwarded to the billerâs financial institution on the business day after they are made if they are submitted to Central 1 by the deadline provided in this section. Payments submitted after the deadline are forwarded to the billerâs financial institution one business day later.
Important
:	Bill payments are subject to cut-off times that vary by submission method or biller. See Section 1.4, Bill Payment Cut-Off Times and Billers with Non-Standard Pricing, for more information.
Financial Institutions that Upload Batch Files
Electronic bill payment batch files must be uploaded to Central 1 by 11 pm PT/2 am ET. Access Central 1âs File and Report Exchange, as described in the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part. Upload batch files to the Bill_Pmt folder using the naming convention provided to the financial institution by Central 1. If the financial institution has Central 1 position monitoring, the batch file submitted canât exceed the set amount or the file will be rejected.
Confirm that the bill payment batch file uploaded successfully. Check that the following files are automatically created in the Bill_Pmt folder a few minutes after uploading the file. The financial institution is responsible for ensuring that the file is successfully received at Central 1. Therefore, always read the EDPR.DAT report and check for a .ERR file.
EDPR. DAT
â Contains a confirmation message advising whether the bill payment batch file was successfully uploaded. If the batch file is successfully uploaded, it is immediately sent to Central 1âs electronic bill payment system.
EDPR.ERR
â If an error in the batch file is identified, Central 1 creates a .ERR file in addition to the .DAT file. The reason for rejection is provided in the .DAT file. Correct the error and resend the bill payment batch file to Central 1.
BPXXXX.BAK
â Is a copy of the original bill payment batch file.
The bill payment files (.BAK files), confirmations (.DAT files) and error notifications (.ERR files) are available on the File and Report Exchange for 30 days. These files are saved in the Backup subfolder of the Bill_Pmt folder. The following naming conventions apply to these archived files:
BPXXXX.BAK.yyyymmdd_hhmm
EDPR.DAT.yyyymmdd_hhmm
EDPR.ERR.yyyymmdd_hhmm
Multiple batch files can be sent during the same day, but the files must be submitted in consecutive file creation number order. Files will reject if
they arenât sent in file creation number order;
the header and trailer records are invalid or not included; or
the item counts or dollar amounts donât balance to the totals provided in the trailer record.
Financial institutions arenât required to send NULL bill payment files.
Financial Institutions that use RFS/NT
If the financial institution uses the RFS/NT banking system, CGI uploads electronic bill payment files to Central 1. CGI consolidates the electronic bill payment files it receives from participating financial institutions and forwards the consolidated files to Central 1 by 3 pm PT / 6 pm ET on business days. If the total file amount doesnât equal the sum of each financial institutionâs file, the file will be rejected. Central 1 will advise CGI, and CGI will attempt to correct the problem. If the problem isnât resolved, CGI will contact the financial institution on the following business day.
7.4	Validating Electronic Bill Payments Sent via File Transmission
All bill payments are validated when they are received at Central 1 regardless of whether the financial institution pre-validated the transactions.
Central 1 rejects transactions that
fail the billerâs account number validation routine;
are duplicate payments (i.e., two or more transactions made to the same biller, under/to the same biller account number, for the same amount from the same customer account number, within a 24-hour period that ends daily at midnight); or
exceed the single transaction limit set for the financial institution.
Important
:	Central 1 wonât reject duplicate payments made to personal and corporate CRA billers that donât accept bill payment reversal requests. The complete list of CRA billers that donât accept same-day bill payment reversal requests is available
here
.
If Central 1 rejects a bill payment, the transaction is reported on the BPRT, Bill Payments Rejected Transaction Report. See Chapter 9, Electronic Bill Payment Reports, and Chapter 11, Reconciliation of Electronic Bill Payments.
Note:	Transactions can be rejected by the biller even if they pass Central 1âs validation process.
END
